intro: Perfoliata villosa, also known as hairy puccoon, is a flowering plant species in the balsam family that is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress.
